get.summary: Lists every account on the shard.
get.description: Passwords are never returned, by any account route.

post.description:
Username and password are required, and the username must be free — a taken one answers 409. Level
is optional and defaults to Player; when sent it must name an account level, case-insensitively.

get.summary: Fetches a single account by username.
get.description: Answers 404 when no account carries that username.

patch.summary: Updates an account, changing only the fields that are sent.
patch.description:
Every field is optional; an omitted one is left as it is. Answers 404 for an unknown account, and
400 when the level does not name one. Returns the account as it stands after the update.

delete.summary: Deletes an account, along with its characters and everything they carry.
delete.description:
Permanent, and the only account route that changes the world rather than just the account record.
An account whose character is currently being played cannot be deleted: the request answers 409
until that character logs out. A 503 means the game loop did not respond and nothing was deleted.

get.summary: Reports the shard's name, build and how many sessions are connected.
get.description: A snapshot taken when the request is served, not a live figure.

get.summary: Lists the plugins this shard activated, each with the HTTP routes it declares.
get.description:
Routes belonging to no plugin — the API reference and framework middleware — are gathered under a
final synthetic entry with id `moongate.host`, so that everything the shard serves appears
somewhere. `policy` names the authorization policy guarding a route, and is null when the
route is open.

post.summary: Trades account credentials for a bearer token.
post.description:
The token carries the account's username and level, and every other route reads authorisation from
it. Send it as `Authorization: Bearer <token>`. Any failure answers 401 without saying
which part was wrong.

post.summary: Exchanges a still-valid token for a fresh one, so an active session does not expire mid-use.
post.description:
Send the current token as `Authorization: Bearer <token>`; the reply has the same shape as
`/api/v1/auth/login`. The new token keeps the `auth_time` of the one presented, so renewing
never restarts the session: past `http.Jwt.MaxSessionHours` from the original login the answer is
401 and the user must sign in again. A 401 also means the account has been deactivated.

get.summary: Every player character on the shard, paged.
get.description:
Ordered by character name. Pass search to filter: it is free text, case-insensitive, and matches
either the character's name or the owning account's username, so "find me so-and-so's character" is
one query. Page is 1-based and defaults to 1; pageSize defaults to 25 and cannot exceed 100. NPCs
are not characters and never appear. A search matching nothing is an empty page, not an error.

get.summary: Opens a per-connection SSE feed; its first event carries the connection id to POST with.
get.description:
Emits `ready` (data = the connection id), then a `line` per command reply and a
            `done` when a command finishes. The connection is closed when the client disconnects.

post.summary: Runs a console command; its reply lines stream to the given connection's SSE feed.
post.description:
Returns 202 immediately — the command is dispatched onto the game loop and its output
            arrives asynchronously on `GET /api/v1/admin/console/stream`.

get.summary: Item art as a PNG, optionally hued.
get.description:
Open without a token: the art is client data every player already has on disk. The id is hex, with
or without the 0x prefix — 0x1234.png and 1234.png are the same item. Pass hue for a coloured
variant; 0, or omitting it, gives the raw art, and the range is 0 to 3000. Images are decoded on
first request and cached, so the first call for an item is slower than every call after it.

get.summary: The facets this shard serves, and the shape of each one's tile grid.
get.description:
Open without a token. Read this before requesting tiles: the facets' sizes come from the shard's own
client files, so maxZoom and the grid differ between shards and must not be hardcoded.

get.summary: A whole facet as one PNG.
get.description:
Open without a token. The entire facet at one pixel per map tile — 6144×4096 for Felucca — for
downloading or printing rather than browsing; use the tiles for a viewer. Optional `?style=`
picks `flat` (default) or `relief` as on the tile route. It is generated once and
cached, and the first request is slow and memory-hungry if the staff pre-warm has not run.

get.summary: One map tile.
get.description:
Open without a token: map data is client data every player already has on disk. The facet is named,
case-insensitively — felucca, trammel, ilshenar, malas, tokuno, termur. Zoom counts up to detail: 0
is the whole facet in one tile, and maxZoom — which GET /api/v1/images/maps reports per facet — is
one pixel per map tile. Tiles are 256×256. A tile at the facet's edge is padded transparent. Tiles
are rendered on first request and cached, so the first call at a low zoom builds everything beneath
it and is slow; the staff pre-warm exists to do that in advance. Optional `?style=` chooses the
render: `flat` (default) is the radar map, `relief` adds altitude shading; each caches on
its own.

post.summary: Builds every map tile and whole-facet image into the cache.
post.description:
Answers 202 and works in the background; poll this same route with GET for progress. Only one runs
at a time, so a second request while one is going answers 409. Worth running before anyone opens a
viewer: the first request at a low zoom otherwise builds every tile beneath it, and the first
request for a whole facet renders a facet-sized image. Progress is kept in memory and does not
survive a restart.

get.summary: Reports how far the map image export has got.
get.description:
State is Idle before any export has run, then Running, and finally Completed or Failed. Failed counts
images that could not be produced; the reasons are in the server log.

post.summary: Generates every item's art into the cache.
post.description:
Answers 202 and works in the background; poll this same route with GET for progress. Only one
export runs at a time, so a second request while one is going answers 409. Unhued art only — hued
variants are generated on demand by the public image route. Progress is kept in memory and does
not survive a restart.

get.summary: Reports how far the item image export has got.
get.description:
State is Idle before any export has run, then Running, and finally Completed or Failed. Failed
counts items whose art could not be written; the reasons are in the server log.

get.summary: Every item template, paged.
get.description:
Ordered by template id. Pass search to filter: free text, case-insensitive, matching the
template's id, name, category or any tag. Page is 1-based and defaults to 1; pageSize defaults
to 25 and cannot exceed 100. A search matching nothing is an empty page, not an error.

get.summary: Fetches one item template by id, specs included.
get.description: Ids are case-insensitive. Answers 404 when no template carries the id.

get.summary: Serves a body's idle, front-facing frame as PNG.
get.description:
The body is a decimal graphic id; pass hue for a skin-hued variant. Generated lazily and cached
on disk. A body with no usable animation answers 404. Anonymous on purpose: it is client data
every player already has on disk.

get.summary: Serves a hair (or facial-hair) style as PNG, rendered over a reference body.
get.description:
The style is the hair item's decimal graphic id (see /api/v1/admin/hair-styles). Pass hue to dye
it, body to change the reference body (default 400, the human male), facial=true for beards.
404 when nothing decodes for the pair.

get.summary: Serves a mobile template's dressed figure as PNG: body, hair and worn equipment.
get.description:
Hue specs on the template resolve to their low end so the image is stable and cacheable. 404 on
an unknown template id, or when the template's body has no animation.

get.summary: Every classified body, paged, for the picker.
get.description:
Classification comes from mobtypes.txt; Equipment-type bodies never appear. Search matches the
decimal id or the 0x-prefixed hex. Ordered by body id.

get.summary: The selectable hair (or facial-hair) styles, paged.
get.description: Pass facial=true for beards. Search matches the style name, case-insensitively.

get.summary: Serves the template's classic client paperdoll as PNG: background, body, hair, equipment.
get.description:
Pass background=false to drop the backdrop gump. Hue and gender-random specs on the template
resolve deterministically so the image is stable and cacheable. 404 on an unknown template id or
when its body has no paperdoll gump.

post.summary: Generates every classified body's image into the cache.
post.description:
Answers 202 and works in the background; poll this same route with GET for progress. Only one
export runs at a time, so a second request while one is going answers 409. Unhued frames only —
hued variants are generated on demand by the public image route.

get.summary: Reports how far the body image export has got.
get.description: State is Idle before any export has run, then Running, and finally Completed or Failed.

post.summary: Creates a news entry, authored by the calling staff member.

get.summary: Lists every news entry, drafts included, newest first.
Returns one news entry in any state.

put.summary: Updates a news entry's title, body and published state.

get.summary: Lists published news, newest first.
Returns one published news entry; 404 if it is missing or a draft.
Reports the account the bearer token belongs to.
get.description:
Username and level, read straight from the token — useful for confirming a token is still valid
and what it grants. It does not list the account's characters.

get.summary: The caller's own characters.
get.description:
Reports every character on the account the bearer token belongs to, with its stats, appearance and
where it stands. Never another account's. The owner field is null here: the caller is the owner. An
account with no characters gets an empty list, not an error.

post.summary: Registers a new account when web registration is open.
post.description:
Answers 403 when registration is disabled, 429 when the caller is rate-limited, 409 for a taken
username, 400 for a missing or malformed field, and 202 on success — the account is created
inactive and must verify its email before it can log in.

post.summary: Verifies an account's email with its token, activating the account.
post.description: Answers 400 for an unknown or already-used token.

get.summary: Returns the shard's public profile: name, description, contacts, asset URLs and whether registration is open.
Streams a visual asset (logo, favicon or banner) by slot.
get.description: Answers 400 for an unknown slot and 404 when the slot has no asset.

get.summary: Returns the full server settings.

put.summary: Updates the server settings; every field is optional and an omitted one is left unchanged.

post.summary: Uploads the image for a slot (logo, favicon or banner), replacing any previous one.
post.description: Answers 400 for an unknown slot, 415 for a non-image type, and 413 when the file is too large.

delete.summary: Removes the image for a slot.

get.summary: Reports the shard's public statistics: players online, accounts, world size and loaded content.
Reports the shard's name and build.
get.description: Open without a token, so a launcher or the website can check compatibility before anyone logs in.

ConsoleCommandRequest.description: A console command to run and the SSE connection its output should stream to.

CreateAccountRequest.description:
A new account. `Level` is the `AccountLevelType` name; omitted means Player, the safe
default — an account that gains staff rights by accident is the wrong way to fail.

CreateNewsRequest.description: Body for creating a news entry; the author is taken from the caller's token.

LoginRequest.description: Credentials posted to the login endpoint.

UpdateAccountRequest.description:
The fields to change. Every one is optional: absent means "leave alone", which is what makes this a
PATCH rather than a PUT. Password is write-only — it enters here and never appears in a response.
Email is not here: the account service has no setter for it.

UpdateNewsRequest.description: Body for updating a news entry.

UpdateServerSettingsRequest.description: A partial settings update: an omitted (null) field is left unchanged.

VerifyEmailRequest.description: Carries the single-use token that verifies an account's email.
